_comment: Surgical seccomp profile for the agent-orchestrator devcontainer. Purpose: let bubblewrap create user/mount namespaces (needed by codex's --sandbox workspace-write and by the orchestrator's future per-attempt bwrap confinement of grok/pi) WITHOUT dropping to --security-opt seccomp=unconfined. Strategy: allow by default, then ERRNO-deny the genuinely host-dangerous syscalls that Docker's default profile also blocks (kernel-module load/unload, kexec, reboot, clock/time changes, raw I/O ports, swap, perf/bpf, etc.). The namespace family (unshare, clone, clone3, setns, mount, umount2, pivot_root, keyctl) is intentionally LEFT ALLOWED. This is not byte-identical to moby's deny-by-default profile; it is an auditable allow-by-default variant chosen for maintainability. Pair with --security-opt apparmor=unconfined (AppArmor docker-default blocks mount/pivot_root). Verify after rebuild: `bwrap --ro-bind / / --unshare-user echo ok`.
